title: Brain, heart, lungs missing: Indian sailor dies in Venezuela, family says major organs removed
content: A shocking discovery has emerged regarding the death of Indian seafarer Rakesh Chauhan in Venezuela. His body, returned to India without vital organs and with extensive prior stitching, has raised grave suspicions. The seafarers' union is demanding a thorough investigation into the alleged foul play and a cover-up, urging immediate intervention from the Indian Embassy for justice and compensation for the grieving family.

title: Hardik Pandya trade: CSK enter the race; seven franchises express interest
content: Seven IPL franchises have expressed interest in signing Hardik Pandya after the all-rounder decided to leave Mumbai Indians. Chennai Super Kings (CSK) have joined Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) and Rajasthan Royals in the race, with talks gathering pace. Player consent, leadership opportunities and trade structures are expected to shape one of the biggest IPL moves in recent years.

title: Cops recreate crime scene, gait analysis of Chetan; dummy used matching Ketan’s weight
content: Pune Rural Police recreated a murder scene at Lohagad Fort, bringing accused Chetan Chaudhary for forensic gait analysis to match CCTV footage. This step aims to verify his involvement in the death of Ketan Agrawal, who fell from the fort on June 18. Investigators suspect a conspiracy involving Agrawal's fiancée, Siya Goyal, and Chaudhary. A fast-track court has been approved for a speedy trial.

title: Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

title: 'HC went beyond ... ': Vijay-led TN govt moves SC challenging cow slaughter ban even on Bakrid
content: Tamil Nadu's government has approached the Supreme Court, challenging the Madras high court's order for a complete ban on cow slaughter. The state argues the high court exceeded its jurisdiction by imposing a blanket prohibition, even on authorized slaughterhouses. Existing laws, they contend, regulate rather than prohibit the slaughter of older, unfit cows, and the government seeks an interim stay on the high court's directive.

title: Court orders new battery for faulty EV scooter, awards Rs 15K compensation
content: A district consumer commission has ordered Okinawa Scooters and its dealers to replace a defective electric scooter battery and speedometer free of charge. The directive comes after the company failed to honor the vehicle's warranty, leaving the owner with a non-functional scooter. The commission also awarded Rs 15,000 for compensation and litigation costs, highlighting the need for proper after-sales service for EV buyers.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: Passport fees, commercial LPG prices, Aadhaar update, ITR deadline: What changes from today
content: Significant financial and policy shifts are now in effect across India. Households and businesses will see changes in fuel prices, rural employment guarantees, and credit card benefits. Aadhaar users can update emails for free, while banks face stricter consumer protection rules. Passport application fees have also increased, and EPFO online services are resuming after an upgrade.

title: 'I don't think he will...': Pujara on Sooryavanshi's India debut in England
content: Cheteshwar Pujara, the former Indian cricketer, offers sage advice to 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, indicating that he may not step onto the field during the upcoming England T20I series. Pujara encourages the young player to remain level-headed amidst the excitement, suggesting that he might find his opportunity only if the seasoned batsmen succeed.

title: No wedding rituals, no valid Hindu marriage despite registration: Gujarat HC
content: A marriage certificate alone cannot validate a Hindu marriage if essential religious rites like Saptapadi were never performed, the Gujarat High Court has ruled. The court emphasized that registration merely proves an already valid marriage, not creates one. This decision nullified a marriage where the woman admitted no rituals took place, despite a registered certificate, highlighting the primacy of religious ceremonies over documentation.

title: USCIS clarifies foreign national registration requirement; Who it applies to
content: USCIS has finalized a new online registration and biometrics rule for certain foreign nationals in the US. This policy, effective since April 2025, targets those lacking standard immigration records. Individuals entering without inspection, certain visa-exempt Canadians, and 14-year-olds in the US must register. The agency assures no new groups are affected, emphasizing procedural updates and traveler-friendly changes to an existing system.

title: FIFA World Cup: Mexico become first team since Italy in 1990 to ...
content: Mexico have achieved a historic feat at the FIFA World Cup 2026, becoming the first team since 1990 to win their opening four matches without conceding a goal. A 2-0 victory over Ecuador secured their first knockout-stage win in 40 years, with Julián Quiñones and Raúl Jiménez scoring. This remarkable defensive and offensive performance positions them as strong contenders as they advance to the Round of 16.

title: Gold price prediction: After worst quarter in 13 years, will gold continue to fall?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices will continue to be under pressure with rate hike expectations in place, with near-term outlook negative, says Vedika Narvekar, Research Analyst - Commodities & Currencies, Anand Rathi Shares and Stock Brokers.

title: Ram Mandir donation case: SIT gets 15-day extension for comprehensive inquiry
content: A special investigation team probing alleged embezzlement of Ram Temple donations in Ayodhya has been granted a 15-day extension. This follows the judicial custody of accused individuals and the resignations of key Trust office-bearers, Champat Rai and Anil Mishra, who took moral responsibility. The Uttar Pradesh police are conducting a high-intensity investigation into financial irregularities, with statements from Trust officials being recorded as the probe continues towards a fair conclusion.

title: Delhi EV Policy 2026: Financial incentives, digital transparency and key changes explained
content: Delhi has officially launched its Electric Vehicles Policy 2026, effective July 1, 2026, aiming to boost EV adoption and improve air quality until March 2030. The policy offers significant tax exemptions for electric cars and substantial subsidies for two and three-wheelers. It also mandates the phasing out of petrol and CNG two-wheeler registrations by April 2028, alongside a Rs 15,000 crore investment and plans for over 30,000 charging points.

title: Two C-17 carrying 66 tonnes of aid: How India mobilised its army to help quake-hit Venezuela
content: India swiftly dispatched a 41-member medical team and 66 tonnes of aid to Venezuela following a devastating earthquake, under 'Operation Amistad'. The team, equipped with advanced medical technology like BHISHM cubes, is providing critical emergency and surgical care. This rapid deployment highlights the Indian armed forces' capability in international disaster relief efforts, earning praise from Prime Minister Modi and gratitude from Venezuelan survivors.

title: Lost your property papers? What you should do to get duplicate copy of lost sale deed
content: Losing your property's sale deed doesn't erase your ownership. If misplaced, approach the Sub-Registrar's office with property details and a written application to obtain a certified copy. If a bank lost your deed, file an FIR, publish a notice, and get a bank affidavit. Lenders might require extra documents for loans when only a certified copy is available.

title: 'Queues at petrol pumps': How Ukraine’s drone strikes are hitting Russia’s fuel supply
content: Russia grapples with a severe fuel shortage, a direct consequence of Ukrainian drone attacks on its oil refineries. This has led to widespread rationing, lengthy queues at petrol stations, and escalating prices, impacting everyday citizens and businesses. President Putin acknowledged the issues, though he downplayed their severity.

title: 'Surya gave me inputs': Shreyas reveals Suryakumar's advice before England series
content: Shreyas Iyer's Team India faces England in a five-match T20I series, aiming to rebound from a surprising loss to Ireland. Iyer emphasized a fearless, aggressive approach, stating the team is starting anew despite past successes. He also shared valuable insights gained from conversations with Rohit Sharma and Suryakumar Yadav, highlighting their support. Iyer also expressed gratitude to doctors for his recovery from a serious injury.

title: Can landlords charge tenants for repainting walls? What Delhi High Court said
content: Tenants aren't always responsible for repainting or minor repairs upon moving out. A Delhi High Court ruling clarified that landlords must distinguish between normal wear and tear, which they cover, and actual damage caused by tenants. While routine painting is the landlord's duty, significant damage like removed fittings can be charged to the tenant, provided the landlord proves it and apportions costs fairly.

title: GST revenue grows 13.9% in June, collections touch Rs 1.94 lakh crore
content: India's GST collections surged 13.9% to Rs 1.95 lakh crore in June 2026, fueled by a significant 34.6% jump in import tax revenues. This robust growth, despite global uncertainties, highlights the maturing and resilient nature of India's tax system. Increased refund disbursements also supported business liquidity, demonstrating improved tax administration and a predictable revenue framework.

title: Gilberto Mora scripts history, becomes youngest to play in WC knockouts since Pelé
content: Teen sensation Gilberto Mora, at 17, became the second-youngest player to start a World Cup knockout match, a feat only Pelé achieved. Mexico triumphed 2-0 over Ecuador, ending a 40-year wait for a knockout victory. Goals from Quiñones and Jiménez secured their Round of 16 spot, marking a historic moment for Mexican football after a dominant performance.

title: CCTV, black bag and a fake 'police' raid: Inside Ayodhya Ram Temple donation theft probe
content: Ayodhya police interrogated Avinash Shukla, an accused in the Ram temple donation theft case, in district jail. Investigators are probing his alleged role in cash counting and reconciliation, suspecting manipulation of records. CCTV footage showing Shukla being escorted with a bag, purportedly containing stolen money, is under examination. Further interrogations of other accused are planned to piece together the alleged embezzlement.

title: Watch: Weeks before Lohagad murder, Ketan Agarwal danced with Siya's father at pre-wedding function
content: A pre-wedding video showing Pune realtor Ketan Agarwal joyfully celebrating with his fiancée Siya Goyal and her parents has gone viral, garnering millions of views. The footage captures a stark contrast to the tragic events that followed, where Agarwal was allegedly murdered by Goyal and her alleged lover. The singer who performed at the event expressed shock and sorrow over the incident, questioning the motive behind such violence.

title: Delhi weather: News of monsoon only comfort amid feels-like 53.5-degree discomfort
content: Delhi is on the cusp of monsoon arrival, with the India Meteorological Department predicting its onset in the next 2-3 days. This comes after the city endured a scorching heatwave, with a 'feels-like' temperature soaring to 53.5 degrees Celsius. While a yellow alert for rain and thunderstorms is in effect, a gradual drop in maximum temperatures is expected over the next week.

title: TNEA result released: 53 candidates secure perfect cut-off, counselling begins July 13
content: The TNEA Rank List 2026 has been released for 2,36,017 engineering aspirants, with Akshitha N of Dharmapuri securing the top rank and 53 candidates achieving a perfect 200 cut-off. Special category counselling will be held on July 13–14, followed by online counselling from July 20 to 30 for admissions across Tamil Nadu.

title: RBI’s net short forward position hits record $106.6 billion amid rupee pressure
content: The Reserve Bank of India's intervention to shield the rupee from volatility, fueled by Middle East tensions, has widened its net short forward position to a record $106.6 billion. Despite a recent dip to a historic low, the rupee has shown recovery, buoyed by anticipated foreign capital inflows. These expected inflows are seen as a potential avenue for the RBI to reduce its forward position and bolster foreign exchange reserves.

title: Pakistani singer Mustapha Zahid reacts as Amaal Mallik recreates ‘Awarapan’ songs
content: Pakistani singer Mustafa Zahid reacted after Amaal Mallik recreated Awarapan's 'To Phir Aao' as the title track for sequel Awarapan 2, calling it his career-best. Mustapha responded warmly, saying "borders are dangerous but so is love." The Emraan Hashmi-starrer, also featuring Disha Patani and Shabana Azmi, releases 14 August.

title: ITR filing: How to claim HRA exemption when filing income tax return - top points to keep in mind
content: ​It’s important to note that the HRA exemption is available only to taxpayers who opt for the old tax regime. It is not an option under the new income tax regime.

title: ‘More important than WC’: Mbappe's tribute to Deschamps after coach’s mother’s death
content: Kylian Mbappé's emotional tribute to head coach Didier Deschamps, who returned after his mother's passing, highlighted France's unity as they secured a World Cup 2026 Round of 16 spot. Mbappé dedicated a goal to Deschamps, emphasizing that 'things are more important than a World Cup.' The team's solidarity deeply moved Deschamps, who praised their unwavering support during his difficult time.

title: 'I won't play him': India's World Cup winner's big verdict on Sooryavanshi
content: England has revealed their playing XI for the first T20I against India, notably excluding Jofra Archer. All eyes are now on India's captain to announce their lineup. Former opener Kris Srikkanth has proposed a strong batting order and spin attack, advocating for the inclusion of Varun Chakravarthy. Srikkanth believes teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi missed an opportunity against Ireland but suggests he won't debut in the initial England matches.

title: Paws over property: Delhi High Court says pets are family, returns dogs to adoptive owners
content: Modifying the orders of the trial court, Justice Girish Kathpalia directed that three rescued pet dogs be returned to their adoptive owners, recognizing the emotional bond shared between the animals and their caregivers.

title: Drones, AI and nuclear submarines: Inside UK’s £298 billion plan to make Britain war-ready
content: The UK is embarking on a transformative defence initiative, investing £298 billion over the next four years. Prime Minister Keir Starmer unveiled this far-reaching plan, which emphasizes the importance of drones, artificial intelligence, and nuclear resilience in response to increasing global tensions. With a focus on modernizing the army and substantial investments in new submarines and combat aircraft, the plan aims to proactively deter threats.

title: As VB - G RAM G comes into force today govt notifies enhanced wage rates: Key details
content: A new rural employment framework, Viksit Bharat- Guarantee for Rozgar & Ajeevika Mission (Gramin), has launched with a significant wage hike. Daily wages now stand at a minimum of Rs 300, with the national average rising to Rs 327.4. This revision, exceeding 10% nationally, prioritizes states with historically lower wages, aiming to boost rural incomes and foster inclusive development.

title: FIFA World Cup Golden Boot race: Kylian Mbappe joins Lionel Messi at the top
content: Kylian Mbappe's stellar performance, including two goals against Sweden, propels France into the World Cup Round of 16. He now shares the Golden Boot lead with Lionel Messi, boasting six tournament goals. Mbappe's remarkable World Cup scoring record continues to grow, solidifying his status as a top match-winner. France's dominant form sets them up for a deep tournament run, with the Golden Boot race intensifying.

title: Why is CA Day celebrated on July 1? History, significance and key facts explained
content: Every year on July 1, India celebrates Chartered Accountants Day to mark the foundation of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) in 1949. The day recognises the invaluable contribution of Chartered Accountants in ensuring financial transparency, ethical governance and regulatory compliance, while tracing the profession's history, significance, ICAI's motto, and the occasion's importance.

title: Calcutta HC directs Bengal to frame guidelines for transporting accused persons
content: There has been a spate of egg attacks on accused persons, particularly local Trinamool leaders, councillors and former MLAs after their arrest since the change of regime in West Bengal

title: 50% traffic e-challan fine waiver nets ₹11.94 crore in 10 days for Bengaluru police
content: According to official data, a total of 3,97,506 traffic e-challan cases were disposed of through the prescribed modes of payment during the 10-day period

title: Census 2027: Enumerators begin door-to-door visits for Houselisting phase in Kerala
content: More than 61,000 enumerators, mostly teachers and other government employees, have been deployed for the exercise. The State has been divided into houselisting blocks for data collection

title: Madras High Court refuses to reject suit against continuance of Nadigar Sangam office-bearers after expiry of term
content: Justice A.D. Maria Clete dismisses an application filed by an association member to reject the suit file by another member

title: Bribery case: CBI arrests IPS officer Deepak Gahlawat
content: The case stems from a CBI trap operation conducted in June, during which a Delhi Police Crime Branch Inspector and an alleged middleman were arrested

title: Tumakuru police crack crude bomb supply network after NH-48 blast
content: The investigation revealed that the explosives had allegedly been procured from George Niklov Fernandes (64) of Chandavar village in Honnavar taluk of Uttara Kannada district

title: Smaller LDF allies in Kerala mull broader platform, keep merger option open after Assembly poll rout
content: NCP faction led by A.K. Saseendran, Congress (Secular) and Indian Socialist Janata Dal exploring formation of a broader platform ahead of possible merger, while remaining part of Left coalition in Kerala

title: DMK seeks FIR over alleged participation of private individuals in Cabinet meetings
content: According to the complaint, John Arockiasamy and Vishnu Reddy, described as “close associates of the Chief Minister and residents of Andhra Pradesh”, had regularly attended Cabinet meetings, official review meetings and other high-level government deliberations held at the Secretariat.

title: Karnataka High Court declines to quash Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act case against associates of US-based Christian missionary organisation The Timothy Initiative
content: The accused illegally used foreign-issued debit cards to channel and withdraw funds in India on behalf of a US-based entity, bypassing regulatory mechanisms

title: Ketan Agarwal murder: Fiancée kept victim’s phone with her for some time
content: According to a police official, the investigation has shown that Agarwal’s mobile phone came into Goyal’s possession after he was allegedly pushed off the cliff at the historical site

title: Man gets life imprisonment for Chilakaluripet murder over extramarital dispute
content: The Narasaraopet court found Akkala Chenchayya guilty of killing Palaparthi Nagaraju in January 2025; proceedings against a juvenile co-accused are pending

title: Hubballi doctors carry out advanced surgery on man suffering severe breathing difficulty
content: Specialist surgeons at HCG Suchirayu Hospital, Hubballi, successfully performed an advanced surgery to treat a 48-year-old patient having a narrowed windpipe resulting in severe breathing difficulties

title: Shrimp farmers shift to local sales amid price drops, export disruptions
content: With exports affected by disruptions in international markets, shrimp farmers have turned to local sales, offering their produce at around ₹400 per kg

title: Assam duo launches digital bird repository with avian identification guide
content: Pokkhi not only seeks to showcase Assam’s avian diversity with the conservation status of each species but also to document their names in all languages spoken by the indigenous communities

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: River waters rise, many roads closed as monsoon rains lash Himachal Pradesh
content: The local meteorological office issued an orange warning for heavy to very heavy rainfall at isolated places from July 2-6, except for July 4.

title: Enumeration Forms in English for GHMC, in Telugu for the rest; dummy forms printed in Urdu for assistance: Telangana CEO office
content: The EFs were distributed to nearly 60% of total 3.38 crore electors in the State, till 2 p.m. on June 30, 2026

title: Darkhast land and the Church of Our Lady of Assumption in Chennai
content: In Urdu, Darkhast means a petition or request, and in East India Company terms it came to mean, particularly in Madras Presidency, grant by the government of poromboke lands to homeless or downtrodden individuals.

title: No complaint by minorities against officials in Ballari district during 18 months
content: Education, healthcare, literacy, skill development and effective implementation of welfare schemes in focus during review meeting on implementation of the Prime Minister’s New 15-Point Programme

title: Kerala Assembly: CM says feasibility study on to set up airstrips in Idukki, Wayanad, Kasaragod
content: Move aims to enable operation of small aircraft and helicopters, considering Kerala’s aviation connectivity and emergency transport needs during natural calamities

title: Kerala to revamp State lotteries, revise prize structure and agents’ commission
content: CM Satheesan’s response in Assembly came after Congress MLA Saneeshkumar Joseph called his attention to challenges faced by lottery sector due to alleged decline in ticket sales

title: Prasoon Joshi-led study group on promoting Indian cinema | Explained
content: The main issue before the Prasar Bharati chairperson-led study group is how to make Indian cinema globally competitive

title: Nimishamba: Chennai’s goddess of instant gratification
content: Was the Nimishamba temple in Chennai built when the first wave of settlers came? Or was it in the second wave when the company induced them to stay?

title: Thin line between defamation and criticism: Delhi HC on Raghav Chadha seeking protection of personality rights
content: It referred to precedent in the Shashi Tharoor case where the court protected the personality rights of the Congress leaders because of his distinctive style of speech and demeanour

title: Why is the Indian Ocean Dipole in the news | Explained
content: With a ‘Super El Niño’ forecast for this year, it is an open meteorological question if a 1997 situation could recur

title: Hindu student held captive, assaulted by unidentified persons in Bangladesh
content: Subhash Deuri, a 25-year-old law student at Jagannath University in Dhaka, was targeted by unidentified persons late on June 30, 2026, according to Bdnews24 media portal

title: Sahitya Akademi Award winner Puviyarasu no more
content: Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay announced that the Tamil Nadu government would accord State honours at the poet’s funeral.

title: ICEYE chooses India for sovereign Synthetic Aperture Radar mission; picks Agnikul Cosmos as launch partner
content: Chennai-headquartered Agnikul Cosmos and ICEYE, the world leader in sovereign intelligence from space, have signed an MoU to explore the potential to build, launch and operate Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) earth observation systems from India

title: Price of commercial LPG, 5-kg FTL and domestic ATF reduced as conflict pressures ease
content: LPG and ATF prices drop as supply improves, while domestic LPG rates remain unchanged amid easing West Asia conflict

title: Kerala Assembly: Opposition LDF walks out over Budget cuts in allocations to local bodies
content: Seeking leave of the House for an adjournment debate, CPI legislator G.R. Anil said the local bodies were facing a serious crisis

title: LPG price hike: Government says rates in India among world's lowest despite 46% jump in global benchmark
content: The government said the cost of supplying a domestic LPG cylinder has risen to more than ₹1,600 following a surge in international prices that followed the outbreak of war in West Asia at the end February

title: BJP rule in Bengal will be no different from rest of country: Venugopal
content: The senior Congress leader also met former editor of The Telegraph, R Rajagopal, who has been denied a passport renewal following the exclusion of his name from the electoral roll

title: Brothers killed as speeding APSRTC bus hits motorcycle near Duvvada in Visakhapatnam
content: P. Satish and P. Girish, of Akkireddipalem, were returning home from Anakapalli when the bus hit their two-wheeler at Vadlapudi Bridge in the early hours of Wednesday

title: MSC-Adani Vizhinjam port deal: Kerala CM says State govt approval needed for share transfer
content: V.D. Satheesan informs Kerala Assembly that Adani Ports has not obtained approval for stake transfer yet. APSEZ had earlier announced that MSC Group will acquire 49% stake in Adani Vizhinjam Port Private Limited for around ₹13,220 crore

title: Singapore delegation inspects housing, lake and tower works in Amaravati
content: During its two-day visit, APCRDA officials explained the progress of the ongoing works, the city’s development strategy and the investment opportunities available in Amaravati

title: Police Sub-Inspector in Bengaluru arrested in POCSO case involving minors
content: The officer is accused of taking the moinor boys, who were detained in connection with a murder investigation, to a lodge, assaulting them and forcing them into performing obscene acts, which he recorded on his mobile phone.

title: The fiscal tightrope for State governments
content: Most State government expenditure is on social sectors, such as health and education, and economic sectors, such as agriculture and irrigation. In Kerala, such spending has driven social progress since the 1960s. The gap between expenditure and receipts is usually financed through market borrowings.

title: Fisheries Development Corporation set for commercial launch of ‘Seafood at Doorsteps’ in Karnataka
content: A dedicated e-commerce platform designed to deliver fresh, hygienically processed and ready-to-cook seafood directly to consumers is expected to create market opportunities for fishing communities in Karnataka

title: Three killed as two lorries collide, catch fire in Andhra Pradesh’s Guntur
content: One of the lorries was transporting stones, while the other was carrying a paper consignment, says Guntur District Superintendent of Police Vakul Jindal.

title: UP defence corridor attracts ₹39,571 cr investment
content: With 65 companies now engaged and nine manufacturing units operational, UPDIC is not only reinforcing India’s commitment to self-reliance in defense but also contributing to Uttar Pradesh’s ambition of becoming a $1 trillion economy.

title: Cabinet expansion plan gathers pace as Karnataka CM D.K. Shivakumar set for Delhi visit soon
content: The proposed expansion is expected to address regional imbalances as well as community and factional considerations, making it one of the most delicate political exercises for the ruling Congress

title: Kerala to implement PM-RAHAT scheme for the treatment of road accident victims soon
content: The initiative, which will be inaugurated at the Medical College Hospital in Thiruvananthapuram by Health Minister K. Muraleedharan, guarantees treatment costs of up to ₹1.5 lakh for a maximum of seven days after an accident.

title: Congress accuses Rajnath Singh of misleading Parliament on Operation Sindoor casualties
content: The Centre’s disclosure exposes a contradiction between the Defence Minister’s statement in Parliament and the official record, the Opposition party says, calling for a full disclosure of facts in the monsoon session

title: K.C. Venugopal moves privilege notice against Rajnath for ‘misleading’ Parliament about Operation Sindoor casualties
content: The Congress leader said that during the discussion held in July 2025 regarding the Pahalgam terror attack and subsequent Operation Sindoor, Rajnath Singh had told the House that no harm had come to Indian soldiers

title: Decks cleared for implementation of ₹12,642-crore VB-G RAM G in Tamil Nadu on July 1
content: As part of the funding arrangement, which is 60:40 for the Union and State governments, the former’s contribution will be ₹ 7,585 crore and the latter’s ₹ 5,057 crore, said an official in the State government.

title: Over 20 lakh devotees attend Rottela Panduga in Nellore
content: On the occasion, State Endowments Minister Anam Ramanarayana Reddy offered special prayers at the dargah

title: Ankita Bhandari case: Uttarakhand HC refuses interim relief to convicts
content: Seeking suspension of sentence and bail, the convicts, Pulkit Arya and Saurabh Bhaskar, argued that Ankita had died by suicide and that there were no direct eyewitnesses to the incident

title: 4-year-old child who fell into borewell in Haryana’s Ambala dies
content: The Deputy Commissioner had said on Tuesday (June 30) that the police had been directed to initiate action as per rules against those responsible for leaving the borewell open

title: Petrol, diesel margins back above pre-conflict levels: Report
content: The start of the West Asia conflict triggered a surge in global oil prices but retail pump rates in India remained steady for large parts and rising only by a fraction

title: Need for inclusive, integrated climate action
content: Climate and heat considerations should be integrated into occupational health policies for municipal and contracted sanitation workers

title: White Paper puts Kerala’s liabilities at ₹5.07-lakh crore, CM says fiscal structure under ‘serious’ strain
content: Salaries, pensions, and interest payments consumed 77.6% of the revenue receipts in 2025-26; a major revamp of KIIFB recommended; urgent reforms sought in KSEB, KSRTC, and KWA; State’s capital expenditure at 1.3% of the GSDP is “one of the lowest” among Indian States

title: "Took 15 Years In Bengal": Revanth Reddy On BJP's 2028 Telangana Power Claim
content: Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy was responding to BJP national president Nitin Nabin's statement that the party would form the next government in the state.

title: Trump Wants One Master List Of Foreign Spies, Intelligence Agencies Say 'No'
content: Some officials believe that putting all these names into one central database could be dangerous, as secret investigations that have been running for years could be exposed.

title: US, Iran To Hold Indirect Talks With Mediators In Doha: Diplomat
content: The talks on Wednesday in Doha will be held with Qatari and Pakistani mediators on the memorandum of understanding.

title: Andhra MLC 'Missing' Amid Probe Into Controversial Remark On Pawan Kalyan
content: Police have not confirmed any arrest or detention and said verification of his movements is underway.

title: Spain Recorded Over 1,000 Heat-Related Deaths In June
content: At least 1,028 people died of heat-related issues during the heatwave, the public Carlos III Health Institute said.

title: AI Models By China, Japan May Have Led To US Lifting Mythos Ban
content: The Trump administration has lifted its export control ban on Anthropics two most powerful models - Mythos 5 and Fable 5.

title: 'World Order Will Collapse': Pakistan's Cry For Help Over Indus Waters
content: One of the strongest remarks came from Pakistani Minister Musadik Malik, who claimed that the future of the treaty would determine the credibility of international agreements worldwide.

title: How Gait Analysis Could Help Crack Ketan Agarwal Murder Case
content: Ketan Agarwal was allegedly pushed into a gorge from the Lohagad Fort near Pune by his fiancee, Siya Goyal, and her lover, Chetan Chaudhary.

title: No Pollution Certificate, No Fuel: Delhi's Rules To Curb Winter Pollution
content: From November 1, motorists without a valid Pollution Under Control (PUC) certificate may not be able to buy petrol, diesel, or CNG.

title: Jet Airways Staff To Get Full Provident Fund, Pension Dues In Setback For SBI
content: The SBI and seven other lenders had argued that provident fund, gratuity, and pension fund dues should form part of the liquidation estate and be distributed among all creditors under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code.

title: Nayara Slashes Fuel Prices, But Why State-Run Oil Firms Are Holding Back
content: So far, there has been no indication from IOCL, BPCL or HPCL that they are preparing to reduce retail fuel prices.

title: US Removes 4 Indian Firms From Sanctions List Over Russia Trade
content: The US had sanctioned Galaxy Bearings Ltd in October 2024, accusing it of exporting dozens of high-priority dual-use equipment, including roller bearings and roller assemblies, to Russian entities.

title: Ukraine's Missiles Now Have Almost Half Of Russia Within Reach
content: Ukraine has deployed new long-range Flamingo and Neptune missiles, triggering alerts across half of Russia and striking targets over 1,500 km away.

title: "Never Going Back To Hormuz": Indian Sailors Fear Returning To Gulf
content: More than three lakh Indian sailors were working in global merchant shipping in 2025, according to the shipping ministry.

title: Japan To Invest $6 Billion In AI, Have 10 Million AI Robots By 2040
content: Countries around the world are seeking to develop sovereign AI models to reduce a potentially dangerous over-reliance on technology from the United States and China.
